rationale: grpc-gateway is a Go module with extensive proto and Go sources; native gazelle (rules_go + go_deps + proto support) is the canonical port path. The repo already vendors generated .pb.go files, so we generate Go libraries and let gazelle map proto deps.
module_bazel:
module(
    name = "grpc_gateway",
    version = "0.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_go", version = "0.61.1")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle", version = "0.51.3")
bazel_dep(name = "rules_proto", version = "7.1.0")
bazel_dep(name = "protobuf", version = "35.1")
bazel_dep(name = "bazel_skylib", version = "1.9.0")
bazel_dep(name = "platforms", version = "1.1.0")

go_sdk = use_extension("@rules_go//go:extensions.bzl", "go_sdk")
go_sdk.download(version = "1.25.4")

go_deps = use_extension("@gazelle//:extensions.bzl", "go_deps")
go_deps.from_file(go_mod = "//:go.mod")
root_build:
load("@gazelle//:def.bzl", "gazelle")

# gazelle:prefix github.com/grpc-ecosystem/grpc-gateway/v2
# gazelle:go_naming_convention import_alias
# gazelle:proto disable_global
# gazelle:exclude docs
# gazelle:exclude examples/internal/browser

gazelle(name = "gazelle")
anticipated_risks: vendored .pb.go vs proto_library generation conflicts (disable_global mitigates)
go:build tools tag file (tools.go) pulling protoc plugins
examples/internal integration tests require network/grpc server at runtime
use_repo list needs bazel mod tidy to populate go_deps
buf-generated swagger/openapi assets not part of Bazel graph
